title: Webhook Subscription Management

story.i_want_to: create, list, update, and delete webhook subscriptions that specify a delivery URL and a list of event types to listen for
story.so_that: my external systems can receive real-time push notifications when state changes occur in loopctl without needing to poll

rationale: While polling via the change feed (US-3.2) works for orchestrators running tight loops, external integrations (CI/CD systems, dashboards, notification services) benefit from push-based delivery. Webhook subscriptions give tenants fine-grained control over which events get delivered where, with per-webhook signing secrets for security and optional project-scoping for multi-project tenants.

acceptance_criteria.description: A webhooks table exists with columns: id (uuid PK), tenant_id (uuid FK), url (string, required), signing_secret_encrypted (binary, Cloak AES-256-GCM), events ({:array, :string}, required, non-empty), project_id (uuid FK, nullable — NULL means all projects), active (boolean, default true), consecutive_failures (integer, default 0), last_delivery_at (utc_datetime, nullable), inserted_at, updated_at.

POST /api/v1/webhooks creates a webhook subscription. Request body: url (required, must be valid HTTPS URL), events (required, non-empty array of valid event type strings), project_id (optional). A signing_secret is auto-generated, encrypted via Cloak, and the raw secret is returned ONCE in the creation response.

GET /api/v1/webhooks lists all webhook subscriptions for the authenticated tenant. Returns id, url, events, project_id, active, consecutive_failures, last_delivery_at, inserted_at. Does NOT return signing_secret.
PATCH /api/v1/webhooks/:id updates a webhook. Updatable fields: url, events, project_id, active. Signing secret is NOT updatable (must delete and recreate to get a new secret).
DELETE /api/v1/webhooks/:id deletes a webhook and all its pending webhook_events.
A tenant cannot create more webhooks than their max_webhooks setting (default 10). Attempting to exceed the limit returns 422 with a descriptive error.

The events field validates against the known event types: story.status_changed, story.verified, story.rejected, story.auto_reset, epic.completed, artifact.reported, agent.registered, project.imported. Unknown event types are rejected with 422.
All webhook CRUD operations require the user role. Agent and orchestrator roles receive 403.
Webhooks are tenant-scoped via RLS. A tenant can only manage their own webhooks.
The signing_secret is generated as a cryptographically random 32-byte hex string and stored encrypted using Cloak's Ecto type.

test_cases.name: create webhook with valid data
expected_results: Response includes id, url, events, active=true, and signing_secret (raw string)
signing_secret is a 64-character hex string (32 bytes)
Webhook record exists in database with signing_secret_encrypted (not plaintext)

test_cases.name: create webhook with invalid event type returns 422
expected_results: Error message indicates invalid event type

test_cases.name: max webhook limit enforced
preconditions: 2 webhooks already created for this tenant
expected_results: Error message indicates max webhook limit reached

test_cases.name: list webhooks does not expose signing secret
expected_results: No webhook object contains signing_secret or signing_secret_encrypted

test_cases.name: update webhook fields
steps: PATCH /api/v1/webhooks/:id with body: {url: 'https://new.example.com/hooks', events: ['story.verified', 'story.rejected'], active: false}
expected_results: events updated to include both event types
signing_secret is NOT returned in the response

test_cases.name: delete webhook cascades to pending events
expected_results: Webhook record no longer exists in database
Associated pending webhook_events are also deleted

test_cases.name: tenant isolation — cannot access other tenant's webhooks
steps: GET /api/v1/webhooks with Tenant A's API key
PATCH /api/v1/webhooks/<tenant_b_webhook_id> with Tenant A's API key
DELETE /api/v1/webhooks/<tenant_b_webhook_id> with Tenant A's API key
expected_results: GET returns empty list (no Tenant B webhooks visible)

test_cases.name: agent and orchestrator roles cannot manage webhooks

technical_notes: Create migration for webhooks table. RLS policies: INSERT/SELECT/UPDATE/DELETE scoped by tenant_id.
Use Cloak.Ecto.Encrypted.Binary for signing_secret_encrypted. Configure Cloak vault in application config.
Known event types should be defined as a module attribute or constant list for validation reuse across webhooks and webhook_events.
Loopctl.Webhooks context module: create_webhook/2, list_webhooks/1, get_webhook!/2, update_webhook/2, delete_webhook/1.
WebhookController with create, index, show, update, delete actions. Routes under /api/v1/webhooks.
URL validation: require https:// scheme (reject http:// in production, allow in dev/test). Validate URL format with URI.parse.
The signing_secret is only returned in the POST creation response. All other endpoints omit it. Use a separate JSON view/serializer for creation vs. list/show.
